mapping.a_element: long-horizon portfolio return covariance across the cross-section of assets
mapping.b_element: squared-exponential covariance kernel of the Gaussian random field

mapping.a_element: eigenstructure / spectral decomposition of that covariance (the spectral generalisation of the variance ratio)
mapping.b_element: Karhunen-Loeve integral eigenvalue problem (IEVP) solved for the kernel's eigenpairs

mapping.a_element: the parsimonious five-factor model (dominant modes retained)
mapping.b_element: the truncated KL expansion (finite set of retained modes)
KL eigenfunctions / basis functions over the spatial domain

mapping.a_element: multi-scale memory components ordered from fast to slow
mapping.b_element: KL eigenvalues ordering modes by variance contribution

brief_justification: Both are built on the spectral (eigen) decomposition of a covariance object into orthogonal eigenmodes retained as a parsimonious truncated set - the Karhunen-Loeve / PCA machinery. Paper A applies it empirically to a long-horizon portfolio-return covariance to recover a five-factor memory decomposition ('eigenstructure of long-horizon portfolio covariance'), while Paper B applies it analytically to a squared-exponential kernel to form a truncated KL basis; the same covariance-eigendecomposition-and-truncation machinery drives both despite the finance vs. Bayesian-inversion framing.
